summary: Monthly community digest feature tested end-to-end. Backend: 9/9 pytest cases pass covering preview, send, idempotency, runs list and RBAC (admin vs resident 403), plus scheduler startup log verification. Frontend: admin sees 'Monthly digest' button (digest-open-btn) on /app/announcements, opening it renders digest-preview with June 2026 label, 0% collection bar, Resolved/Upcoming/Notices tiles and Recent sends list. Send button triggers /admin/digest/send and shows 'already sent' toast on subsequent calls (idempotent for the already-sent month). Non-admins do not see the digest-open-btn.

minor.issue: Uses HTTP POST semantics for a read-only preview; could be GET for cache-ability and semantic clarity.

When dry_run=True on preview call, ALL users are still fetched even though not iterated — minor N+1 avoidance opportunity; also digest_html is not rendered in preview so payload matches API but visual preview cannot be inspected server-side.

Idempotency skip returns {skipped:true, month, ...payload}. Response payload no longer contains sent_count/total_users on skip, which frontend does not need but is inconsistent with non-skipped response shape.

issues: DialogContent missing aria-describedby (existing app-wide a11y warning; non-blocking).
Uses window.confirm() for send confirmation — consider Shadcn AlertDialog for consistency with rest of app.
Preview shows only 3 tiles (Resolved/Upcoming/Notices); pending amount / expenses total from payload are hidden in UI though present in email HTML.

action_items: Optional: expose pending / expenses total tiles in digest preview UI so admins see the full email at a glance.
Optional: swap window.confirm for AlertDialog to match rest of app UX.
Optional: add aria-describedby to digest DialogContent.
critical_code_review_comments: APScheduler starts inside FastAPI @app.on_event('startup') without a lock — if multiple workers were ever spun up (they are not today), each would fire the cron. Consider guarding via db-lock or single-worker deploy note.

send_monthly_digest sends emails sequentially in a for loop (await inside loop). For >100 residents this becomes slow and can block Resend rate limits. Consider asyncio.gather with concurrency limit or BackgroundTasks.

digest_runs insert happens AFTER all sends; if the loop crashes mid-way, the month is never marked sent and next call will re-send to already-emailed users. Consider try/finally to persist even on partial failure.
Idempotency key is just 'month' (YYYY-MM); resend for a specific month cannot be forced by an admin without deleting the digest_runs doc — no admin 'force resend' flag exposed.
_digest_html uses user['name'].split()[0] — will KeyError if a user lacks 'name' (older seed users). Add .get('name','Resident').

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all data-testids resolve; preview + idempotent send flow verified)

seed_data_creation: Test resident user test_res_digest_<ts>@example.com registered by module fixture; no cleanup (kept minimal).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Digest for the current 'last month' (June 2026 as of Jul 21 2026) is already sent and stored in db.digest_runs. To retest a fresh send path, delete the corresponding digest_runs doc. Preview (dry_run=True) is always fresh and can be re-run indefinitely. Note pydantic email validator rejects .test TLD; use example.com for test emails.
verified_features: POST /api/admin/digest/preview returns all required fields (label, month_start/end, collection_pct, invoices_paid/unpaid/total, collected, pending, expenses_total, resolved_complaints, open_complaints, upcoming_bookings[], notices[]) and does NOT create a digest_run.
POST /api/admin/digest/send returns {skipped, month, sent_count, total_users, ...payload} on first send and {skipped:true, month, ...payload} on subsequent same-month calls.
GET /api/admin/digest/runs returns array sorted desc by sent_at with {month,label,sent_at,sent_count,total_users}; no _id leaked.
Resident cannot call any /api/admin/digest/* endpoint — all return 403.
Backend log contains 'Monthly digest scheduler started (cron: day=1 hour=9)' on startup.

Frontend admin /app/announcements shows digest-open-btn; dialog renders digest-preview with June 2026 label, 0% collection bar, Resolved/Upcoming/Notices tiles and 'Recent sends' list (June 2026 — sent to 20/20).
digest-send-btn shows 'already sent' toast when the month has been sent (idempotency verified via UI).
digest-open-btn is not rendered when user is not admin (verified via unauthenticated /app/announcements — button absent).
